meta.title: Mariology 1: An Evangelical Review and Critique of Eastern Orthodox Mariological Orthopraxy
meta.author: Joshua Schooping

questions.question: Which publisher does the speaker identify for the Marian prayer book he reviews?
questions.answer: New Rome Press

questions.question: The introduction attributes many prayers to a corpus once wrongly ascribed to which figure?
questions.answer: St. Ephrem the Syrian

questions.question: How does the introduction describe the actual authors of these Greek prayers?
options: Exclusively known by name in every case
All composed in the 12th century in Russia
Written in Greek by largely anonymous patristic and Byzantine writers
questions.answer: Written in Greek by largely anonymous patristic and Byzantine writers

questions.question: Which learned Patriarch of Constantinople does the speaker cite as evidence some texts are quite old?
questions.answer: Photios

questions.question: What approximate age does the speaker infer for many of the prayers?
options: Over a thousand years in many cases
questions.answer: Over a thousand years in many cases

questions.question: The speaker connects hyperdulia toward Mary with which other Orthodox practice?
questions.answer: Icon veneration as material mediation

questions.question: Which saint does the speaker name as author of a Marian canon included after the thirteen prayers?
questions.answer: Theodore the Studite

questions.question: The opening prayer’s first line, as quoted, says Mary alone is what?
options: Wholly pure in soul and body
questions.answer: Wholly pure in soul and body

questions.question: The speaker juxtaposes Mary as sole dwelling place of the Spirit’s fullness with which New Testament theme?
options: Believers as temples of the Holy Spirit after Pentecost
questions.answer: Believers as temples of the Holy Spirit after Pentecost

questions.question: Which line does the speaker highlight as refusing even to ask God to heal sin?
options: I will ascend to the throne alone
I do not dare ask the loving God to heal my many sins
Forgive us our debts as we forgive
questions.answer: I do not dare ask the loving God to heal my many sins

questions.question: Which soteriological term does the speaker say is applied to Mary in the prayers (turning away wrath)?
questions.answer: Propitiation

questions.question: The speaker claims Christus Victor themes are largely absent from these prayers; what does he say Orthodox apologists often emphasize regarding the atonement?
questions.answer: Christus Victor

questions.question: Prayer 4 applies which Gospel image to Mary that the speaker says properly belongs to Christ?
questions.answer: True vine

questions.question: Which shocking phrase does the speaker quote about Mary and the divine essence?
options: You are lower than the cherubim
You contain the entire essence of the divinity
You are without honor in your own country
questions.answer: You contain the entire essence of the divinity

questions.question: The speaker cites Mary as addressed with which exclusive mediatorial title for the world?
options: A witness to the covenant only
The mediator for the whole world
questions.answer: The mediator for the whole world

questions.question: In Prayer 5, what does the suppliant say about approaching Christ?
options: I run boldly to the throne
I do not dare draw near with boldness to your son
questions.answer: I do not dare draw near with boldness to your son

questions.question: The speaker describes Theodore’s canon as primarily what kind of structure?
options: A dialogue cycling sinner, Mary, Christ, and Mary again across eight odes
A single uninterrupted hymn to the Trinity
A legal treatise on canon law
questions.answer: A dialogue cycling sinner, Mary, Christ, and Mary again across eight odes

questions.question: In the canon, how does the speaker characterize Christ’s initial posture toward the sinner?
options: Unwilling to forgive and provoked to wrath
questions.answer: Unwilling to forgive and provoked to wrath

questions.question: Near the end of the canon, Christ ties possible mercy to what, according to the speaker?
options: Mary’s essence shared with the Father
questions.answer: Fruits of repentance

questions.question: The speaker compares Mary’s perpetual intercessory role to what modern metaphor?
options: Helicopter mothering at a divine level
questions.answer: Helicopter mothering at a divine level

questions.question: Which Psalm-related image does the speaker say is applied to Mary in a troubling way?
options: Hyssop and cleansing / whiter than snow
questions.answer: Hyssop and cleansing / whiter than snow

questions.question: The after-communion prayer asks Mary, as mother of the source of immortality, to do what for the suppliant?
options: Create life in me who lie dead in sin
questions.answer: Create life in me who lie dead in sin

questions.question: What does the speaker claim is confessed in Prayer 9’s frame?
options: Sins confessed to Mary as to the primary hearer
questions.answer: Sins confessed to Mary as to the primary hearer

questions.question: The speaker ends by listing which cluster of Reformation slogans?
options: The four marks of the church only
Christ alone, faith alone, grace alone, Scripture alone, God’s glory alone
questions.answer: Christ alone, faith alone, grace alone, Scripture alone, God’s glory alone

questions.question: What football analogy does the speaker use for gradual assimilation into Marian piety?
options: Moving down the field by degrees toward the end zone
questions.answer: Moving down the field by degrees toward the end zone
